query: What Con Edison commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge in Manhattan based on OpenEI tariffs data?
final_answer:
I analyzed all Con Edison ("Consolidated Edison Co-NY Inc," EIA ID 4226) Commercial tariffs in the OpenEI IURDB that serve a Manhattan address (1 Centre St, New York, NY 10007). Manhattan falls in Con Edison's **NYC / Zone J** territory, so I compared the demand-charge components (flat demand `$/kW` rates plus their adjustments, and any time-based demand rates) across all the Zone J / NYC service classes.

## Answer: SC‑8 — Multiple Dwellings Redistribution, Low Tension Service (Zone J / NYC)

This class carries the **highest demand charge of any Con Edison commercial service class in Manhattan**, at roughly **$68.54/kW** in the peak demand block:

- Base flat-demand rate: **$53.85/kW** + adjustment **$14.69/kW** = **≈ $68.54/kW** (a lower first block of $41.56 + $14.69 ≈ $56.25/kW also applies, with the first 10 kW at $0).
- Source: OpenEI IURDB record "SC-8 - Multiple Dwellings Redistribution Low Tension Service - Zone J [NYC]" (label `67b6667e54c99cee7203bf9b`), Con Edison, EIA ID 4226, effective 2025.

### How it compares to the other Manhattan (Zone J/NYC) commercial classes

| Service class (Zone J / NYC) | Peak demand charge (rate + adj) |
|---|---|
| **SC‑8 Multiple Dwellings Redistribution – Low Tension** | **~$68.54/kW** (53.85 + 14.69) |
| SC‑12 Multiple Dwellings Space Heating – Low Tension | ~$66.77/kW (60.19 + 6.58) |
| SC‑8 Multiple Dwellings Redistribution – High Tension | ~$53.73/kW (38.70 + 15.03) |
| SC‑9 General Large – Low Tension | ~$53.14/kW (42.80 + 10.34) |
| SC‑12 Space Heating – High Tension | ~$51.46/kW (44.93 + 6.53) |
| SC‑9 General Large – High Tension | ~$39.65/kW (29.50 + 10.15) |
| SC‑13 Bulk Power Housing Developments | up to ~$27.72/kW (27.36 + 0.36) |
| SC‑2 General Small | no demand charge (energy + fixed only) |

(All figures from the OpenEI IURDB Consolidated Edison Commercial tariff records returned for the Manhattan address, EIA ID 4226, 2025 effective rates.)

### Key assumptions / caveats
- **"Manhattan" = Con Edison NYC / Zone J.** Con Edison's posted tariffs are zonal (NYC vs. Westchester, with sub-zones H, I, J). Zone J is the New York City zone covering Manhattan, so I used the Zone J / NYC variants. The plain "[NYC]" (non-zone) variants of the same classes carry lower adjustment factors and thus lower totals.
- These OpenEI records reflect the **Delivery** (and "Delivery with Standard Offer") portion of the bill; demand is billed in **$/kW**. I combined the base demand `rate` with its `adj` (reconciliation/MAC-type adjustment) to get the all-in demand charge, and took the highest applicable block.
- If you restrict the comparison to **demand charge alone** and a true "general service" class (rather than the multiple‑dwelling redistribution classes), **SC‑9 General Large – Low Tension (Zone J)** at ~$53.14/kW would be the highest standard large-commercial demand charge.

**Bottom line:** Among Con Edison commercial classes serving Manhattan in the OpenEI tariff data, **SC‑8 (Multiple Dwellings Redistribution, Low Tension Service), Zone J** pays the highest demand charge, at approximately **$68.54/kW**.

steps.reasoning: I'm looking up the OpenEI tariff data for Con Edison's commercial rates in Manhattan.

I need to identify which Con Edison commercial customer service class has the highest demand charge in Manhattan, which is Zone J. I'm looking at the demand charge structures—both flat rates and time-of-use rates—to find the maximum, and I'm noticing SC-12 Space Heating Low Tension as a candidate to examine.

Computing the demand charges with adjustments for the NYC Zone J records...

steps.reasoning:
I'm looking at the demand charge rates for Con Edison's commercial service classes in Manhattan, specifically comparing the flat demand structure rates measured in $/kW across Zone J to identify which class has the highest charge.

Scanning through the data, SC-8 Low Tension Zone J shows the highest flat demand charge at 68.54 $/kW (53.85 base plus 14.69 adjustment), though SC-12 Space Heating Low Tension comes close at 66.77 $/kW. I should also check the time-of-use demand rates to see if any of those exceed the flat rates I'm finding.
